title: Rolling-Dough Robust Planning: Saddle-Point Update Schedule Under a Round Budget
strategies: naive constant gradient descent-ascent at the supplied conservative reference step (beta = gamma = 0); reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline and scores ~0.1
greedy step tuning: keep pure descent-ascent but sweep a 1-D grid of constant step sizes and take the best; converges faster than the conservative baseline but the rotational coupling caps it

constant optimistic-GDA + Polyak momentum tuned by a deterministic 3-scalar grid search over the exact round budget; optimism cancels rotation and momentum accelerates the strongly-monotone contraction, driving the residual far below baseline while staying short of the per-round-tuned optimum

per-round (non-constant) schedule design toward the Krylov/Chebyshev-optimal degree-K polynomial method (e.g. GMRES-style least squares over M^j(z0-z*)); the analytic ceiling that still leaves headroom below 1.0 on every instance
